DISCOVERY 610, DEFENDANT ASTRAZENECA PHARMACEUTICALS' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AS TO KATHRYN SCHULTZ IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANT SERVING SUBPOENAS FOR MEDICAL RECORDS. PARTIES AGREE THAT DEFENDANT MAY SUBMIT INTERROGATORY TO IDENTIFY MEDICAL PROVIDERS. RESPONSE DUE WITHIN 15 DAYS. RECORDS SHALL BE PRODUCED INITIALLY TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF PURSUANT TO AUTHORIZATION. COUNSEL MAY REDACT THE RECORDS. PRODUCTION BY JANUARY 25, 2008. IF ANY DISPUTES REGARDING REDACTIONS, THE COURT WILL CONSIDER AN EX PARTE APPLICATION TO CONDUCT AN IN CAMERA REVIEW. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS ASTRAZENECA PHARMACEUTICALS, L.P., ASTRAZENECA, L.P., JANSSEN, L.P., JANSSEN PHARMACEUTICA INC., AND JOHNSON & JOHNSON'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE (TO SUTTER COUNTY). RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE TO SUTTER COUNTY GRANTED. NO TRIAL DATE IS SET AND DISCOVERY IS ONGOING. THE MOTION IS TIMELY AND TRANSFER WOULD NOT PREJUDICE PLAINTIFFS. DEFENDANTS' DECLARATIONS SUFFICIENT TO DEMONSTRATE THE CONVENIENCE OF WITNESSES AND THE ENDS OF JUSTICE WOULD BE PROMOTED BY THE CHANGE. SUTTER COUNTY PHYSICIANS WILL SAVE TIME AND EXPENSE WITH LESS ADVERSE IMPACT ON THEIR PRACTICES BY GRANTING THIS MOTION. PERCIPIENT WITNESSES ARE LOCATED IN SUTTER COUNTY. NO COUNTER - DECLARATIONS SUBMITTED BY PLAINTIFFS. ORDER ON MOTION TO TRANSFER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. IN ADDITION, THE COURT SIGNED DEFENDANTS "ORDER RE EXHIBIT B TO DECLARATION OF DENNIS J. CANTY FILED IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFFS' OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE." (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion To Transfer Venue
Off Calendar Oct-28-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS ASTRAZENECA PHARMACEUTICALS, L.P., ASTRAZENECA, L.P., JANSSEN, L.P., JANSSEN PHARMACEUTICA INC., AND JOHNSON & JOHNSON'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE (TO SUTTER COUNTY).
APPEARANCES: Dennis J. Canty, Esq. of Levin Simes Kaiser & Gornick LLP 273-8138 for pltf & opposing party; Peter A. Strotz, Esq. of Filice Brown Eassa & McLeod LLP 510/444-3131 for defts & moving parties ASTRAZENECA PHARMACEUTICALS, L.P., ASTRAZENECA, L.P.; Steven M. Selna, Esq. of Drinker Biddle & Reath LLP 591-7500 for defts & moving parties JANSSEN, L.P., JANSSEN PHARMACEUTICA INC., AND JOHNSON & JOHNSON.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE TO SUTTER COUNTY GRANTED. NO TRIAL DATE IS SET AND DISCOVERY IS ONGOING. THE MOTION IS TIMELY AND TRANSFER WOULD NOT PREJUDICE PLAINTIFFS. DEFENDANTS' DECLARATIONS SUFFICIENT TO DEMONSTRATE THE CONVENIENCE OF WITNESSES AND THE ENDS OF JUSTICE WOULD BE PROMOTED BY THE CHANGE. SUTTER COUNTY PHYSICIANS WILL SAVE TIME AND EXPENSE WITH LESS ADVERSE IMPACT ON THEIR PRACTICES BY GRANTING THIS MOTION. PERCIPIENT WITNESSES ARE LOCATED IN SUTTER COUNTY. NO COUNTER - DECLARATIONS SUBMITTED BY PLAINTIFFS. Order on motion to transfer signed in open court. In addition, the Court signed defendants "order re exhibit B to declaration of Dennis J. Canty filed in support of plaintiffs' opposition to defendants' motion to transfer venue." (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses From Kathryn Schultz
Off Calendar Dec-11-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
Catherine V. Barrad, Esq., and Sean Commons, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Astrazeneca Pharmaceuticals, L.P. and Astrazeneca, L.P.
Laura Brandenberg, Esq., and Dennis Canty, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Kathryn Schultz and Robert Schultz

HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant Astrazeneca Pharmaceuticals' motion to compel further responses to request for production of documents as to Kathryn Schultz is Denied without prejudice to defendant serving subpoenas for medical records. The parties are ordered to meet and confer as to limiting the scope of any subpoenas to a reasonable period of time before the death of plaintiff's son and as to those conditions at issue.
Parties agree that defendant may submit interrogatory to identify medical providers. Response due within 15 days. Records shall be produced initially to counsel for plaintiff pursuant to authorization. Counsel may redact the records. Production by January 25, 2008. If any disputes regarding redactions, the Court will consider an ex parte application to conduct an in camera review. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses From Robert Schultz
Off Calendar Dec-11-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
Catherine V. Barrad, Esq., and Sean Commons, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Astrazeneca Pharmaceuticals, L.P. and Astrazeneca, L.P.
Laura Brandenberg, Esq., and Dennis Canty, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Kathryn Schultz and Robert Schultz

HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant Astrazeneca Pharmaceuticals' motion to compel further responses to request for production of documents as to Robert Schultz is Granted in part as to the financial documents. Denied without prejudice to defendant serving subpoenas for medical records. The parties are ordered to meet and confer as to limiting the scope of any subpoenas to a reasonable period of time before the death of plaintiff's son and as to those conditions at issue.
Parties agree that defendant may submit interrogatory to identify medical providers. Response due within 15 days. Records shall be produced initially to counsel for plaintiff pursuant to authorization. Counsel may redact the records. Production by January 25, 2008. If any disputes regarding redactions, the Court will consider an ex parte application to conduct an in camera review. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.
